evals.prompt: Explain the 3-state audit flag system. What does each state mean for /think and /absorb?
evals.expected_output: Describes true/false/manual flags with their effects on other skills
expectations: Describes true flag: verified, no warning on /think, auto-absorb
Describes false flag: unverified, warning on /think, blocked on /absorb
Describes manual flag: re-review, warning on /think, approval prompt on /absorb

evals.prompt: What are the 3 modes of the verify.py script? Show the commands.
evals.expected_output: Run verify, check status, set flag — with script commands
expectations: Shows verify.py command for running a verification
Shows --status flag for checking verification status
Mentions fuzzy name matching for book selection

evals.prompt: How does the verification generate and evaluate questions?
evals.expected_output: 50 fresh questions across 3 types, scored at >=95
expectations: States 50 fresh questions (not reused from self-learn)
Mentions topic-based, cross-cutting, and adversarial question types
States 100% pass leads to audited: true, any fail to audited: false
